title: carpinus betulus subsp. serrata
intro: Carpinus betulus subsp. serrata, also known as the Serrate Hornbeam, is a subspecies of deciduous tree in the family Betulaceae. Traditionally, the Serrate Hornbeam has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The bark and leaves of the plant are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. Carpinus betulus subsp. serrata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing individuals to harness its potential medicinal and health benefits.
